picks.reason: Atlanta has the better full-game shape against a small-sample Robert Gasser, but Bryce Elder’s weak underlying skill and Atlanta’s taxed late-inning group keep the Braves price from clearing my side gate. My run estimate lands above 8.5, but the playable Over price is flagged suspect and I do not want the Under in a warm day game with two strong platoon offenses.

picks.reason: Chase Burns is the better starter, but the Yankees’ home edge and Cincinnati’s still-light team quality leave the side near fair and short of my threshold. My estimated total is 9.8: the Giants project well against Gusto, Miami’s bullpen is strained, and even with a likely roof-driven neutral environment this number still comes out comfortably above 8.

picks.reason: Washington has the better offensive profile and Tampa’s starter looks more beatable than his ERA, but Andrew Alvarez is still a small-sample arm and that uncertainty suppresses any side aggression. The total market is absent to me because both listed prices are flagged suspect.

Houston’s offense and bullpen freshness are the separators, but the available favorite price is close to my fair number and not enough of a bargain. I project this total modestly above the market, mainly from two merely average starters and Cleveland’s less-fresh relief mix, but the edge is just below my betting threshold.

St. Louis has the cleaner side profile: better offense versus right-handed pitching, the stronger underlying starter in Dustin May, and a slight late-game edge despite both bullpens carrying some usage noise. I make the Cardinals 58.7% to win, enough to clear the -120 price, while my total projection is only slightly above 9 and not enough for a wager in a gusty, variance-adding environment.

picks.reason: San Diego’s starter is listed TBD, which makes this an automatic full-game pass under my rules. I do not estimate either side or total without confirmed opposing starter data.

Pittsburgh has the better side setup, but Coors inflates scoring and margin volatility enough that the favorite price does not qualify as a clean edge for me. My total projection is above 12, but the only playable listed total side is the Under, so the over market is effectively absent.

Reid Detmers is pitching well enough to make the Angels attractive on paper, but the Athletics’ strong split versus lefties and the hitter-friendly park offset much of that edge, leaving the dog short of my betting threshold. I project this total above the posted 9.5, but the available playable number is only on the Under side.
picks.data_gap: confirmed Athletics lineup versus left-handed pitching

picks.reason: Los Angeles is the right side on talent and bullpen shape, but the ML is too expensive for the actual gap and Dodger Stadium is not a park where I want to force a run-line condition. My total runs projection is above 9, yet the only clean total price available is the Under, so I pass both markets.

Logan Gilbert and the park give Seattle the base case, but Boston’s lefty starter is good enough to keep the Mariners from clearing my edge gate at the available price. The total market is absent because the shown prices are flagged suspect, and this is already a low-run environment where number quality matters a lot.

Zack Wheeler is the biggest starter edge on the board, but the Phillies are priced close to that reality already and I do not chase a heavy favorite without extra discount. My estimated total is 8.8, so the Over is close, but Philadelphia’s weaker split versus lefties and Wheeler’s ability to suppress one side keep it to a lean only.
